data.description:
## Context
Part of the exclusion/exception determination build-out (epic: Data Source Hierarchy, GH https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/issues/732). Hand-off pickup for **Jeff**, week of 2026-07-13. Plan: `Tech Specs/OSCER/exclusion-determination-service-buildout-plan.md` (Track A / A2). **This is the architectural spine** both Track A and Track B (Jake's data-source checks) converge on.

Today exclusion and exception live in two separate services (`ExclusionDeterminationService`, `ExceptionDeterminationService`) run at two business-process steps. Per the 07-08 design thread, the target is a single ordered-checks engine that evaluates checks in priority order and, for each, can emit an exclusion and/or an exception result: the same condition (e.g. pregnancy) is an **exclusion** if active now and a mandatory **exception** if it lapsed during the lookback. A1 makes those signals date-aware; A2 consumes them.

## Design (from the 07-08 thread; NOT loop-and-collect)
- `ordered_checks`: each check exposes `highest_priority_result` (the best result it *could* return) and `determine(certification)` returning 0-2 results (exclusion and/or exception).
- **Skip gate:** run a check only if its `highest_priority_result` can beat the current best. This is the same cost-mitigation the spec describes for data-source calls. Track to the winner, categorize exclusion vs exception, emit the right event.
- **Priority = lowest number wins** (ascending), and there is **one shared ordering where every exclusion outranks every exception**. Reuse the merged `Exclusion.priority_order` config (https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/pull/758 / https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/issues/751); keep the existing `priority` field naming (no rename this pass).
- member_data-backed checks first (Jeff endorsed); Track B's data-source-backed checks plug into the *same* `ordered_checks` list later, so this interface is the seam.
- The existing `ExceptionDeterminationService` (`EXCEPTION_CHECKS` symbol array to per-check month-intersection methods, lazy first-wins) is the concrete pattern to generalize: each of its checks becomes an ordered check exposing a priority + a result.

## Naming note (open, do NOT block on it)
The plan proposed renaming `ExclusionDeterminationService` to `ExemptionDeterminationService` to reflect dual emission. Per Mike (2026-07-10) **keep names as-is for now**: build the ordered-checks + dual-emission behavior under the existing service and treat the rename as a deferred cosmetic decision, not a requirement of this ticket.

## Sequencing / preconditions
- **Depends on A1** (date-aware signals) and **#761** (return-best single-winner selection is the core this generalizes).
- Consumes the shared month-intersection helper (shared-kernel note; coordinate with #760).
- Spec not yet formally approved: orchestration bakes in exclusions-first / skip-lower-durability / audit-all-return-winner. Sumi cleared best-vs-all (07-07); confirm the rest before finalizing (open decision #3 in the plan).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A single ordered-checks engine evaluates checks in ascending priority with a skip gate (a check runs only if its best-possible result can beat the current best).
- [ ] A check can emit an exclusion and/or an exception; the engine categorizes the winner and publishes the correct event(s) (`DeterminedExcluded` / `DeterminedExcepted` and the not-* counterparts), preserving current audit-log writes.
- [ ] Exclusions and exceptions share one comparable ordering; every exclusion outranks every exception.
- [ ] Existing exclusion-only and exception-only outcomes are preserved for member_data-only inputs (regression net over the current two-service behavior).
- [ ] The `ordered_checks` interface is documented as the extension point Track B data-source checks plug into.
- [ ] `make lint` clean; `make test` green (SimpleCov 92/70 gate).

## Sources
- Plan: `Tech Specs/OSCER/exclusion-determination-service-buildout-plan.md` (Track A / A2)
- 07-08 design thread: https://nava.slack.com/archives/C0A4VQW8K0R/p1783525315306859
- Spec: Data Source Hierarchy section "Exceptions from former exclusions"; siblings #757 / #765 / #760; return-best #761
data.title: A2 — Ordered-checks determination engine emitting exclusion + exception (spine)
